title: Chicken Pie
excerpt: Chicken pie is something we all know from our childhood. It was a Sunday staple on our table. You can make chicken pie from ready-cooked shop chicken, but nothing beats chicken you've cooked yourself with love. We're going to eat the chicken pie with just salads, because Hopefield stands still today …
html:
Chicken pie is something we all know from our childhood. It was a Sunday staple on our table. You can make chicken pie from ready-cooked shop chicken, but nothing beats chicken you've cooked yourself with love. We're going to eat the chicken pie with just salads, because Hopefield stands still today from the heat. ( 42 °C) I made a big chicken pie. For 12 people. You can halve the recipe or make 2 pies and freeze one.

YOU NEED:

  • 2 whole chickens
  • 1 onion
  • 250 ml white wine
  • 250 ml water
  • 6 peppercorns
  • 2 bay leaves
  • 6 cloves
  • 2,5 ml ground nutmeg
  • 60 ml lemon juice
  • 1 packet bacon
  • 2 tins creamed mushrooms
  • 1 puff pastry dough
  • salt and pepper
  • 2 hard-boiled eggs
  • 60 ml sago soaked in 125ml water

NOW:

  1. cut the chicken into smaller pieces, trim off all the loose skin and fat and put the chicken in a pot
  2. add the onion, bay leaves, peppercorns, nutmeg, cloves, water and wine to the chicken and cook for about 60 minutes – until the chicken is done and comes loose from the bones.
  3. shred the chicken and keep 250 ml of the stock it was cooked in
  4. fry the bacon until brown
  5. put the chicken back in the pot with the bacon, lemon juice, sago and the 250 ml stock.
  6. cook it through, salt and pepper and taste what else is needed
  7. place the chicken in an oven dish
  8. slice the boiled eggs and place on top of the chicken
  9. I put a pie bird in my chicken, it stops the dough from sagging
  10. roll out the dough and make a little cross in the dough where it will go over the pie bird
  11. I still need to bake my pie and will post the photo after Christmas
  12. press down with a fork and bake for 30 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius ,
